sections_cited: Section 4 of the Sindh Service Tribunal Act

chunks.text: Order Sheet IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR C. P. No. D – 751 of 2023 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge Fresh case 1. For orders on office objections at Flag-A 2. For orders on CMA No.3081/2023 (Ex./A) 3. For hearing of main case 02.05.2023 Mr. Khan Muhammad Sangi, Advocate for the petitioner..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- The petitioner was initially appointed as Sub-Engineer (BPS-11) on 30.09.1993 and presently working as Assistant Engineer (BPS-17) in Works & Services Department, Government of Sindh. He preferred many appeals including one dated 09.02.2023 to Chief Secretary, Government of Sindh, Karachi against notification dated 24.02.2016, which have not yet been decided; hence, through instant petition, he seeks directions to Chief Secretary, Government of Sindh to decide his departmental appeals/ representations of the various dates. Needless to mention here that the remedy is available to the petitioner for redressal of his grievance under Clause (a) of Section 4 of the Sindh Service Tribunal Act, 1973 in case the appeal/representation of the petitioner was not decided by the competent authority within a period of ninety days before the Service Tribunal concerned; however, instead of being approached to the Service Tribunal, the petitioner preferred to repeat his appeals/representations including last one dated 09.02.2023 before the authority concerned. We, therefore, dismiss this petition along with listed application being not maintainable in law, leaving the petitioner at liberty that in case his appeal/representation is not decided within the stipulated period by the competent authority, the petitioner may approach to the Service Tribunal in accordance with law for redressal of his grievance.

We, therefore, dismiss this petition along with listed application being not maintainable in law, leaving the petitioner at liberty that in case his appeal/representation is not decided within the stipulated period by the competent authority, the petitioner may approach to the Service Tribunal in accordance with law for redressal of his grievance. J U D G E J U D G E Abdul Basit
